#  Move Drawings to Another Drawing Area 
##  Background 
If your project uses [drawing areas](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-drawing-areas), you might run into a scenario where drawings need to be moved from one drawing area to another. Instead of deleting and re-uploading the drawings, you can use Edit to move them to another area. See [Edit Drawings](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/edit-drawings) or follow the steps below.
##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawings** tool.
  2. Mark the checkboxes next to the drawings you want to move.
  3. Click **Edit.**
  4. Select the dropdown next to Drawing Area Field.
  5. Choose whether you want to move the drawings to an existing area or create a new one:
     * **To move drawings to an existing drawing area** :1. Begin typing the name of the drawing area, or double click into the field to view a list of existing drawing areas.2. Select the drawing area from the drop-down menu.
     * **To move drawings to a new drawing area:** **a)** Enter a name for the new drawing area in the 'Drawing Area' field. **b)** Click Create Area. 


_Note:_ The drawing area name must be less than 255 characters, and the name cannot already exist in the project.
6. Click **Update**.
#####  Notes
  * Drawings will be in the 'Unpublished' state after being moved to a new area. To publish drawings, see [Publish Drawings](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/user-guide-bidding-and-estimating-integration/publish-drawings).
  * If one or more drawings has the same drawing number as a drawing in the existing area, you are prompted to confirm the drawings on the review page.
  * Email notifications are sent in the following scenarios:
    * If more than 50 drawings are being moved at one time, you will receive an email when the drawing move is complete.
    * If drawings were unable to be moved, you will receive an email with one or more error messages.
  * Drawing hyperlinks may take a couple minutes to appear after the drawings have been confirmed.


## See Also
  * [Edit Drawings](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/edit-drawings)
  * [What is the difference between copy and duplicate with drawing markups?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-difference-between-copy-and-duplicate-with-drawing-markups%3F)
  * [Compare Drawing Revisions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/compare-drawing-revisions)
